info.name: FixIt API Tests
info.description:
Generated from synced FixIt routes.php and frontend api.js. Import with the FixIt Local Environment. Secure-channel endpoints are included with plaintext payload references and header placeholders; they require the app's X25519/AES-GCM/HMAC wrapper to execute successfully.

Encryption: routes with SecureChannelMiddleware (Bookings, Wallet, Stripe, KYC, chat messages, profile update) need a real X25519 + HKDF + AES-256-GCM + HMAC envelope that Postman's sandbox can't compute natively. Use the companion `secure-channel-helper.js` script in this folder (run with Node.js) to compute the exact headers/body to paste in, or let it perform the call directly and show the decrypted response — see each secure request's description for a ready-to-run example command.

if (pm.response.text() && ct.includes('application/json')) { pm.test('response is valid JSON', function () { pm.response.json(); }); }

if (json.user) pm.environment.set('last_user_id', json.user.id);

request.description:
SECURE CHANNEL REQUIRED: this route has SecureChannelMiddleware. The app encrypts/signs the body with X25519 + HKDF + AES-256-GCM + HMAC. GET has no HTTP body, so the client sends the encrypted payload in an X-Sec-Body header instead. Plain JSON is included only as the pre-encryption payload reference; it will not pass unless wrapped by the secure transport.

To actually run this request, compute the encryption with the companion script in this folder (Postman's sandbox has no native X25519 support): `node secure-channel-helper.js --token <bearer_jwt> --method GET --path /bookings/1` — prints ready-to-paste headers/body, and can also perform the live call itself so you can see the decrypted response directly.

exec: pm.test('response is not a server error', function () { pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.be.below(500); });
const ct = pm.response.headers.get('Content-Type') || '';
if (pm.response.text() && ct.includes('application/json')) { pm.test('response is valid JSON', function () { pm.response.json(); }); }

request.description:
SECURE CHANNEL REQUIRED: this route has SecureChannelMiddleware. The app encrypts/signs the body with X25519 + HKDF + AES-256-GCM + HMAC. Plain JSON is included only as the pre-encryption payload reference; it will not pass unless wrapped by the secure transport.

To actually run this request, compute the encryption with the companion script in this folder (Postman's sandbox has no native X25519 support): `node secure-channel-helper.js --token <bearer_jwt> --method POST --path /bookings --body '{"provider_id": "1", "category_id": "1", "scheduled_at": "2026-07-10 10:00:00", "address": "123 Postman Street", "notes": "Postman booking", "recurrence_type": "none", "recurrence_end_date": null, "provider_service_id": "x", "coupon_code": "x"}'` — prints ready-to-paste headers/body, and can also perform the live call itself so you can see the decrypted response directly.

exec: pm.test('response is not a server error', function () { pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.be.below(500); });
const ct = pm.response.headers.get('Content-Type') || '';
if (pm.response.text() && ct.includes('application/json')) { pm.test('response is valid JSON', function () { pm.response.json(); }); }

request.description: Starts the dynamic secure channel. client_pub must be a base64 raw X25519 public key generated by the client.

exec: pm.test('response is not a server error', function () { pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.be.below(500); });
const ct = pm.response.headers.get('Content-Type') || '';
if (pm.response.text() && ct.includes('application/json')) { pm.test('response is valid JSON', function () { pm.response.json(); }); }
if (pm.response.code >= 200 && pm.response.code < 300) { const j = pm.response.json(); if (j.session_id) pm.environment.set('secure_session', j.session_id); }
pm.test('status is 2xx', function () { pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.be.within(200, 299); });
const ct = pm.response.headers.get('Content-Type') || '';

request.description:
SECURE CHANNEL REQUIRED: this route has SecureChannelMiddleware. The app encrypts/signs the body with X25519 + HKDF + AES-256-GCM + HMAC. GET has no HTTP body, so the client sends the encrypted payload in an X-Sec-Body header instead. Plain JSON is included only as the pre-encryption payload reference; it will not pass unless wrapped by the secure transport.

To actually run this request, compute the encryption with the companion script in this folder (Postman's sandbox has no native X25519 support): `node secure-channel-helper.js --token <bearer_jwt> --method GET --path /jobs/1/messages` — prints ready-to-paste headers/body, and can also perform the live call itself so you can see the decrypted response directly.

SECURE CHANNEL REQUIRED: this route has SecureChannelMiddleware. The app encrypts/signs the body with X25519 + HKDF + AES-256-GCM + HMAC. Plain JSON is included only as the pre-encryption payload reference; it will not pass unless wrapped by the secure transport.

To actually run this request, compute the encryption with the companion script in this folder (Postman's sandbox has no native X25519 support): `node secure-channel-helper.js --token <bearer_jwt> --method POST --path /payments/stripe/customer` — prints ready-to-paste headers/body, and can also perform the live call itself so you can see the decrypted response directly.

exec: pm.test('response is not a server error', function () { pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.be.below(500); });
const ct = pm.response.headers.get('Content-Type') || '';
if (pm.response.text() && ct.includes('application/json')) { pm.test('response is valid JSON', function () { pm.response.json(); }); }
